title: Daily Report June 24, 2026 - GoThru Photographer API

issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 15/06/26

✅ Implementation Complete Update GetPhotographersDashboard Sorting — Ready for Unit Testing

Objective:
Modify the GetPhotographersDashboard API to prioritize photographers with a "pending" status ( status = 1 ) by placing them at the top of the returned results.

Changes Implemented:

  • Updated the GORM query's Order clause in src/action/v1/photographers/getPhotographersDashboard.go .
  • Replaced the simple chronological sort ( created_at DESC ) with a conditional SQL CASE statement.
  • The new sorting logic: CASE WHEN status = 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, created_at DESC .
    • Primary Sort: Evaluates the status field. Records with status = 1 are assigned a lower sorting weight ( 0 ), forcing them to the top of the query result. All other statuses are assigned a higher weight ( 1 ).
    • Secondary Sort: Falls back to sorting by created_at DESC , ensuring that within the "pending" group (and the subsequent non-pending group), the newest entries still appear first.
  • This approach is chosen because it is cross-compatible across different SQL database engines (such as PostgreSQL and MySQL) without breaking the existing pagination implementation ( Limit and Offset ).

Photographer Dashboard Pending First

Date: 2026-06-24

Request: Update /v1/photographers/users/dashboard so pending photographer
accounts appear first in the dashboard list.

1. Background

The Featured Photographers dashboard is used by support/admin users to review
photographer accounts and take action on their status.

Today, accounts waiting for review can be buried across many pages. This creates
extra manual work because the reviewer must scan page after page to find the
accounts that need attention.

The screenshot feedback is clear: pending accounts should be visible first
because they are the work queue.

2. User Problem

Support users need to review pending photographer accounts quickly.

They do not have time to go through many pages of denied, approved, or older
accounts just to find the accounts that are still waiting for action.

When pending accounts are hidden deeper in the list:

  • Reviews take longer.
  • Pending accounts may be missed.
  • Account approval or denial is delayed.
  • The dashboard feels harder to use than it needs to be.

3. Desired Experience

When the photographer dashboard opens, pending accounts should appear at the top
of the list by default.

After pending accounts are shown, the rest of the list can continue in the
normal order, with the newest accounts first.

The dashboard should still feel familiar. This is not a redesign. It is a better
default order that matches the reviewer's priority.

4. Recommended Product Direction

Use Pending first as the default dashboard order.

This means:

  • Pending photographers appear before denied, approved, and featured
    photographers.
  • Within the pending group, the newest pending accounts appear first.
  • After pending accounts, other accounts continue in newest-first order.
  • Existing search behavior remains the same.
  • Existing status filter behavior remains the same.
  • No new button or training is required.

This is the simplest change that directly solves the user pain.

5. Alternatives Considered

Option A - Keep Current Order

This keeps the dashboard unchanged, but it does not solve the problem. Support
still has to search through multiple pages to find pending accounts.

Option B - Add a Manual Pending Filter

This helps, but it still requires the reviewer to remember to use the filter.
The problem is urgent enough that the dashboard should show pending accounts
first automatically.

Option C - Create a Separate Pending Review Queue

This could be useful in the future, especially if the review process becomes
larger. For now, it is more than needed. The current dashboard can solve the
main problem by showing pending accounts first.

Recommended Option

The existing filter can remain available, but the default view should keep the
familiar dashboard layout with one important change: pending accounts are
prioritized first.

6. Success Criteria

The change is successful when:

  • A reviewer can open the dashboard and immediately see pending accounts on the
    first page when pending accounts exist.
  • Reviewers no longer need to scan many pages to find pending accounts.
  • Search and status filters continue to work as users expect.
  • The dashboard still feels like the same dashboard, only easier to review.

7. Out of Scope

This brainstorm does not require:

  • A full dashboard redesign.
  • A new review workflow.
  • New status labels.
  • New buttons or navigation.
  • Changes to how reviewers approve, deny, or log in as photographers.

8. Plain-Language Acceptance Criteria

  • When pending accounts exist, they show before non-pending accounts.
  • Pending accounts are ordered with the newest pending accounts first.
  • Non-pending accounts still appear after pending accounts.
  • If the user searches, the results still match the search.
  • If the user selects a status filter, the dashboard still respects that filter.
  • The reviewer can find pending accounts without going through many pages.
